description_long: This object consists of two wooden storage crates, one of which is open with its lid removed and leaning against the side, while the other remains closed. The crates are constructed from planks of dark, weathered wood, reinforced with diagonal and horizontal wooden slats for added strength. The wood grain is clearly visible, and the planks are fastened together with metal rivets at the corners and along the lid. The open crate reveals a hollow interior, suitable for storing or transporting various items. The lids are flat and removable, with no visible hinges or locking mechanisms. The overall appearance suggests a rugged, utilitarian design commonly found in urban storage areas, garages, or outdoor settings.
description: A pair of rugged, dark wooden storage crates, one open with a removable lid and the other closed, featuring visible wood grain and metal rivets.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open crate with its lid removed and leaning, and the closed crate behind it.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back panels of both crates and the detached lid resting on the ground.
description_view_2: Angled view showing the closed crate in the foreground and the open crate behind, with the lid visible to the side.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the open crate's interior and the flat, removable lid propped up nearby.
